_note: The each header's `as` keyword may be surrounded by any whitespace (newline/tab/CRLF), not just single spaces — upstream parses the iterable with read_expression, then allow_whitespace + match('as'). A newline before `as` used to silently drop the as-clause, leaving `item` unbound and treating `items\nas item` as the iterable expression (phantom TS1005 errors).
